meta.method: Read all 5 reports, normalized findings, deduped into a pooled union, verified every pooled finding against the reference files (parallel region-scoped verification agents + direct spot-checks of every disputed verdict). Note: several first-pass verdicts mislabeled confirmed contradictions as INVALID; each was re-checked directly against the files and corrected.

invalid_findings.claim: documents/index.md FR-MAINT row summary disagrees with SRS FR-MAINT.
invalid_findings.evidence: index.md:72 says 'Automated project maintenance via deno task check'; requirements.md:72 FR-MAINT Description says the SAME ('automated project maintenance via deno task check'). They agree; no divergence.

invalid_findings.claim: Orphan [x] FRs implemented by md skills lack SALP/code back-reference.
invalid_findings.evidence: AGENTS.md:130-137 explicitly permits non-code evidence (acceptance tests, file/dir existence) placed in SRS; the cited FRs (fr:ship, fr:doc-tasks, etc.) carry acceptance-test evidence, so they are policy-compliant, not defects. fable-high gate-dropped the same lead as false.

invalid_findings.claim: codex.ts:57 outputFormat='stream-json' overloads Claude's literal; mis-routes logs.
invalid_findings.evidence: format_logs.ts intentionally treats 'stream-json' as an NDJSON-family tag and explicitly supports Codex's event schema; codex logs parse correctly (pinned by codex_test.ts). Documented intentional design, not a contract defect.

invalid_findings.claim: adapters/version.ts cliVersion() returns '' as in-band sentinel conflating probe-failure — a smell.
invalid_findings.evidence: version.ts:1-11 JSDoc documents the empty-string-on-failure / never-throws contract as deliberate, chosen for a stable cache key. fable-high and opus-medium both verified this as intentional design and dropped it.
